yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday's 1.5mi open water swim at San Diego (45 TSS) was a solid aerobic session that maintained swim fitness while keeping load appropriate for taper week. The workout aligns perfectly with the reduced volume strategy (-60%) heading into race week, and open water exposure is valuable race prep with Oregon 70.3 just 13 days out.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's 40-minute easy-moderate ROUVY ride (NP under 140w) followed by the 15-minute brick run exactly as planned. Focus on neuromuscular activation and race feel, not fitness building—keep the bike NP well under 140w and run at conversational pace.
weekProgress: Week 12 of 13 is tracking perfectly with 0 TSS of 200 target after just one day. This is completely expected given the back-loaded structure—Monday's brick session will add meaningful TSS, and the schedule appropriately emphasizes sharpness over volume in this true taper week before the drive to Salem.
keyInsight: You're in textbook taper mode 13 days out from Oregon 70.3—the focus has correctly shifted from building fitness to preserving freshness while maintaining race-specific feel through short bricks and quality swim sessions.
positives: Sleep rebounded strongly to 83/100 with 7.8h duration after the poor 41-score night on July 4th
Last week's training demonstrated excellent discipline with appropriate taper volume (TSS 327 across 6 sessions)
